headline: France's dilapidated National Museum of Natural History calls for help: 'The mineralized bones literally are exploding'
description: The institution boasts strong visitor numbers and an international scientific reputation. But its head warns of catastrophic conditions, including mold in the collections, a flooded library, and cracks in the walls.

headline: Trump extends ceasefire but continues blockade
Extreme weather a growing threat to elections and democracy
Belgian PM's troubling ambivalence toward Russia
description: Bart De Wever faces criticism for his unclear stances on the level of support the European Union should provide to Ukraine. The Flemish nationalist has defended himself against accusations of playing into Moscow's hands, insisting that he only aims to push the 27 member states forward.

headline: Who is John Ternus, Tim Cook's successor as Apple CEO?
description: A hands-on leader known for his calm management style, Apple's future CEO must now prove his ability to drive innovation.

headline: World Cup fans face soaring prices as transport costs skyrocket in the US
description: Massachusetts and New Jersey transit authorities have announced dramatic fare hikes for round-trip train journeys to stadiums. The move has drawn sharp criticism from fans, already grappling with steep costs to attend the tournament.

headline: Why colonial-era Congo archives in Belgium are caught in a standoff over strategic minerals
description: Accusations of 'digital colonialism' and capitalist predation are at the heart of a standoff between an American mining company and a Belgian public museum, as interest in the Democratic Republic of Congo's vast mineral wealth intensifies.

'Why am I alive?': The Yazidi genocide as told by one of its survivors
description: Samih Taha, now a refugee in France, is one of the few survivors of the Islamic State group massacre of his village in northern Iraq on August 15, 2014. Then 17 years old, he went into debt to buy back his mother and sisters, who had been forced into sexual slavery. He told Le Monde his story and the tragedy that befell his family.

headline: France's print media in crisis amid massive layoffs
description: Since December 2025, magazine and regional press companies have announced nearly 1,000 job cuts, putting the viability of countless newsrooms at risk.

headline: Discontent with Putin rises among part of the Russian population: 'There is an immense wall between you and us'
description: Internet shutdowns, a struggling economy and the ongoing war in Ukraine have exasperated some Russians, while a recent poll shows the president's popularity is slipping.
